title: Westpac lifts rates for second time in three weeks
full_text: Other Editions 24 April 2026 MarketWatch Westpac lifts rates for second time in three weeks The Australian Cameron Micallef 24 April 2026 397 words English AUSTLN 23 © News Pty Limited. No redistribution is permitted. Westpac has become the latest of the big four banks to increase fixed rates as households brace for further mortgage pain. On Thursday, Westpac lifted its fixed-rate offering for the second time in three weeks as the bank prepared for further rate hikes. Thursday’s hike was 0.15 per cent across each of its one, two, three, four and five-year offerings. As a result, Westpac’s fixed interest rates now start from 6.29 per cent, available on its two-year offering. Overall, Westpac has lifted interest rates by 45 basis points in the past three weeks. However, even with the two interest rate hikes in three weeks, Westpac’s fixed rates remain the cheapest of the major four banks. Canstar data insights director Sally Tindall said Westpac’s interest rate rise wasn’t an isolated event. “Plenty of banks are heading back to the pricing drawing board, some for the second time in a matter of weeks, as a result of growing concern that Australia’s inflation rate is about to spike once again,” she said. “Our analysis shows more than 90 per cent of lenders have moved to fixed rates since the last RBA decision, including all four big banks, with both Westpac and NAB having two stabs at it in this time.” According to Canstar, just 19 lenders are offering at least one fixed rate under 6 per cent. This time last year there were 83. “For borrowers, this swift repricing is a clear signal – opportunities to lock in a more competitive fixed rate are slipping away,” Ms Tindall said. Westpac is lifting its fixed rate offerings to customers as it predicts further interest rate pain for households. In a previously released forecast, Westpac predicted there would be three more interest rate hikes in 2026. Westpac chief economist Luci Ellis said the Middle East conflict is flowing through to fuel prices, which will eventually lift the broader inflation rate. “We now expect the RBA to hike in June and August as well as May,” Ms Ellis previously said. Australia’s inflation rate has re-accelerated lately as the US/Israel and Iran war, which started at the end of February, led to the blockage of a critical waterway known as the Strait of Hormuz. summary: Westpac has increased its fixed interest rates for the second time in three weeks, raising rates by 0.15% across all fixed terms. The bank's rates now start at 6.29% for a two-year fixed loan, with a total increase of 45 basis points in this period. This trend reflects broader concerns among lenders about rising inflation and potential further rate hikes in 2026.

key_points: Westpac raises fixed rates by 0.15% for the second time in three weeks
Current fixed rates start from 6.29% for two-year loans
Over 90% of lenders have adjusted fixed rates amid inflation concerns
relevance: Understanding interest rate movements is crucial for actuaries and financial services professionals as they impact lending, investment strategies, and economic forecasts.
